toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. The user wants to:
1. Run the tests
2. Collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process

For .NET test crash dumps, I can use the `dotnet test` command with the `--blame-crash` option, which collects a crash dump when the test host process crashes. This is a built-in feature of the .NET test platform.

Let me run the tests with `--blame-crash`.

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-14113c6389f1482188ae33d5f6635c68 && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command used:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector, which will automatically capture a crash dump (via `procdump` / `createdump`) if the test host process crashes unexpectedly. Since no crash occurred here, no dump was generated. If a crash does happen, the dump file will be written to the `TestResults/` directory alongside a sequence file showing which test was running at the time.
